evaluator.note: Benchmark v2. Blind subagent: packet-only context (prompt.md, diff-drift-report.md, raw-git-diff.patch); dispatched by the maintainer's agent session on 2026-06-10. Answer recorded verbatim.

findings.title: CORS origin allowlist replaced with wildcard on admin API
findings.evidence: Raw diff confirms `origin: ["https://admin.example.com"]` was replaced with `origin: "*"` in the exported corsOptions. This removes the single-origin allowlist for a repo named admin-api, allowing any website to make cross-origin requests to admin endpoints. The branch is agent-authored (`agent/fix-cors`), and widening to `*` is a trust-destroying shortcut rather than a fix — the correct change would adjust or extend the allowlist.

notes: Diff Drift's flag matches the raw git diff exactly (1 changed file, 1 flag), so the report is corroborated and not over- or under-reporting on this packet.

The report's detail line 'credentials can leak' is slightly overstated for this specific snippet: no `credentials: true` option is visible in the diff, and browsers reject `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` combined with credentialed requests. However, some CORS middleware reflects the request origin when configured permissively, and the wildcard exposure of an admin API to arbitrary origins is blockable on its own regardless of the credential nuance.

Whether `block` vs `investigate` depends on intent: if the agent was asked to open the API to additional origins, the right remediation is adding them to the allowlist, not `*`. Either way the change should not be trusted as-is.
